get.description: Serve the machine-readable security.txt per RFC 9116.
Human-readable responsible-disclosure policy (JSON).
Hall of fame for security researchers.

schema.title: Response Security Acknowledgments Security Acknowledgments Get

get.description:
Return metadata for a single trace id within a recent window.

This mirrors the aggregation done by `/traces/recent` but scoped to a single
`trace_id`. It searches calls and legacy diagnosis jobs within the last
`days` and returns a `TraceListItem` describing the trace.

post.description: Record thumbs-up / thumbs-down signal for an Ask Zroky answer.

Encrypt and persist a provider key for this project.

Behaviour:
  - 422 on bad provider vocab or empty/short plaintext.
  - 409 if a key with the same fingerprint is already active for
        this provider in this project (re-paste of the same key).
  - 503 if `PROVIDER_KEY_VAULT_KEK` is unset/short — cipher refuses
        to write plaintext.
  - On success the previously-active row for (project_id, provider)
    is automatically revoked so only the new row is active.

get.description:
List provider keys for the calling tenant.

By default returns only active keys (is_active=true). The dashboard
uses include_revoked=true on the audit/history view to render
rotated keys.

parameters.name: provider

schema.description: Filter by provider; 422 if not in VALID_PROVIDERS
schema.title: Provider

parameters.description: Filter by provider; 422 if not in VALID_PROVIDERS

parameters.name: include_revoked

schema.description: When true, also returns is_active=false rows
schema.title: Include Revoked

parameters.description: When true, also returns is_active=false rows

get.description: Fetch a single key's metadata. 404 if missing or cross-tenant.

delete.description:
Revoke a key. Idempotent — calling on an already-revoked row
returns the same row unchanged. 404 if missing or cross-tenant.

schema.title: Response Github Webhook V1 Integrations Github Webhook Post
Response List Diagnoses Alias V1 Diagnoses Get
Response List Projects V1 Projects Get
Response List Api Keys V1 Projects  Project Id  Api Keys Get
Response List Project Memberships V1 Projects  Project Id  Memberships Get
Response List Project Diagnosis Shares V1 Projects  Project Id  Diagnosis Shares Get

post.description: Invite a user to a project by email. If they already exist, add them directly.

Dispatch a replay run for a golden set.

Creates a `ReplayRun` row in `pending` status. Actual replay execution
is handled by a background worker (deferred to a later module). Clients
should poll `GET /v1/replay/runs/{id}` for status.

get.description:
Return the calling tenant's monthly replay quota state.

Accessible to all plan tiers (no feature gate) so the dashboard can
show the correct upgrade prompt to Free and Starter users.

post.description:
Dispatch a replay run via the GitHub-Action-friendly surface.

Resolution order for the target golden set:
  1) explicit `golden_set_id` in the body (must belong to the tenant)
  2) project's `default_golden_set_id` (Module 9 / migration 0060)

Returns 422 when neither is available, 404 when the resolved set
no longer exists for the tenant, and 202 on accept.

Kick off a regression-CI run. Returns 202 with the run_id; the
actual work proceeds in a BackgroundTask.

Validation:
  * `git_sha` required (4-64 chars).
  * `threshold` in [0, 1].
  * `operator_override.category` must be a known blast-radius.

The synthetic GoldenSet is upserted on first call per project so
the FK-bound ReplayRun row is creatable without a separate
onboarding step.

get.description:
Fetch the current status + (when terminal) full report for a run.

Status semantics:
  * `queued`         — accepted, not started yet.
  * `running`        — orchestrator is mid-loop.
  * `pass`/`fail`    — terminal, report + comment available.
  * `error`          — terminal, summary may still be partially populated.

Tenant isolation: a run from another project is reported as 404 so
the existence of the run_id is not leaked.

post.description:
Mark a tier-1 applied action as reverted. The actual config
rollback is handled by a downstream worker (deferred to a later
module). Returns 409 if the action is not in `applied` state or is
not tier-1.

Cancel a `pending` tier-2 action — the founder console uses
this to intervene before the auto-PR opens.

Returns:
  404 if the action does not exist for this tenant.
  409 if the action is tier-1 (use /revert) or not in 'pending'
      state (already applied / failed / skipped).

Re-dispatch a previously `failed` or `skipped` Tier-2 action.

Reads the original anomaly + replay_run_id_gate off the action
row and re-runs `evaluate_tier2_dispatch` from scratch. Because
the dispatcher always writes a fresh row, this never mutates the
original action; the response describes the *new* action.

Requires the original action to be tier-2 and in one of the
non-applied terminal states. Tier-1 retries flow through the
Tier-1 revert/apply worker (not this endpoint).

Returns:
  404 if the original action doesn't exist for the tenant.
  409 if the action is tier != 2 or already in pending/applied.
  422 if the original action is missing the gate evidence
      (replay_run_id_gate is NULL) — happens on actions skipped
      BEFORE the gate was checked (e.g. entitlement gate).

get.description:
Return the project's pilot policy. First read seeds the §6.3
canonical default so the dashboard always has a row to render.

put.description:
Replace the project's pilot policy. Pydantic enforces the simple
type + range constraints (booleans, ge=0, ≤1, etc.); the service
layer re-validates and trims string entries before persisting.

get.description:
Read-only Intel Pulse feed.

422 on any malformed filter (unknown kind/severity, oversize source/
model, undecodable cursor, expired cursor row).

post.summary: Upsert the current user's vote on a coming-soon feature.
post.description:
Create or update the current user's vote.

One vote per (subject, feature_key). Voting again overwrites
the previous answer. `use_case` is optional free-text (max 2000
chars) — encouraged when `vote == 'interested'`.

get.summary: Get the current user's vote on a coming-soon feature.
get.description:
Return the vote, or 404 if the user has not voted yet.

Also returns 400 if the feature_key is not in the registry — same
behavior as POST, so the dashboard can render the same error.

get.summary: Summary of all coming-soon features at a glance.
get.description:
Returns one summary per registered feature_key.

Includes features with zero votes so the dashboard can render a
consistent table.

get.summary: Summary + recent votes for a single feature_key.
get.description:
Returns the aggregate summary + recent_votes list.

Filters:
  • vote   = 'interested' | 'not_interested' (optional)
  • since  = ISO-8601 timestamp (optional, only votes after)
  • limit  = 1..500 (default 100)

get.summary: Export all votes for a feature_key as CSV.
get.description:
Stream a CSV of every vote on this feature_key.

Columns:
  created_at, updated_at, vote, user_email_masked, user_subject,
  project_id, project_name, use_case

post.description:
Start a Stripe Checkout session for a self-serve plan.

Errors:
  422 — bad plan_code (out-of-vocab OR not self-serve, e.g. 'free' or 'enterprise')
  503 — BILLING_ENABLED=false OR Stripe Price ID missing for the plan
  502 — Stripe API error (live gateway only)

Return a Stripe Customer Portal session URL.

Errors:
  404 — org has no Stripe customer yet (must hit /checkout first)
  503 — BILLING_ENABLED=false
  502 — Stripe API error

Stripe webhook receiver.

Auth: HMAC-SHA256 via the `Stripe-Signature` header (NOT a tenant
header). Stripe expects HTTP 200 on success; we return 400 only for
signature failures (Stripe will retry on 5xx, which is what we want
for transient handler errors).

Behaviour:
  - Verifies signature against STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET.
  - Idempotent claim via `stripe_events.stripe_event_id` UNIQUE.
  - Dispatches to `services.stripe_sync.dispatch_event`.
  - Records every event (handled or skipped) in `stripe_events`
    for audit.

get.description:
Return the calling org's current plan + Stripe identifiers.

Auto-creates a free-tier shell `subscriptions` row on first call so
the dashboard never sees a 404 here. The plan_template field is the
canonical baseline for the current plan; Module 6's resolver may
override individual values via override/trial rows.

  project_id, project_name, use_case

schema.description: Must be 'DELETE_CONFIRMED'

post.description:
Start a Stripe Checkout session for a self-serve plan.

Errors:
  422 — bad plan_code (out-of-vocab OR not self-serve, e.g. 'free' or 'enterprise')
  503 — BILLING_ENABLED=false OR Stripe Price ID missing for the plan
  502 — Stripe API error (live gateway only)

Return a Stripe Customer Portal session URL.

Errors:
  404 — org has no Stripe customer yet (must hit /checkout first)
  503 — BILLING_ENABLED=false
  502 — Stripe API error

Stripe webhook receiver.

Auth: HMAC-SHA256 via the `Stripe-Signature` header (NOT a tenant
header). Stripe expects HTTP 200 on success; we return 400 only for
signature failures (Stripe will retry on 5xx, which is what we want
for transient handler errors).

Behaviour:
  - Verifies signature against STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET.
  - Idempotent claim via `stripe_events.stripe_event_id` UNIQUE.
  - Dispatches to `services.stripe_sync.dispatch_event`.
  - Records every event (handled or skipped) in `stripe_events`
    for audit.

get.description:
Return the calling org's current plan + Stripe identifiers.

Auto-creates a free-tier shell `subscriptions` row on first call so
the dashboard never sees a 404 here. The plan_template field is the
canonical baseline for the current plan; Module 6's resolver may
override individual values via override/trial rows.

post.description: Create a new project invitation (owner or admin).

get.description: List invitations for a project (owner/admin).

schema.title: Response List Invitations Api V1 Invitations Projects  Project Id  Invitations Get
Response Revoke Invitation Api V1 Invitations Projects  Project Id  Invitations  Invitation Id  Delete

post.description: Accept an invitation using the raw token.

Response List Diagnosis Pr Links Api V1 Diagnosis  Diagnosis Id  Prs Get

AdminAllFeatures.title: AdminAllFeatures
AdminAllFeatures.description: All registered features at a glance for the owner dashboard.

AdminFeatureDetail.title: AdminFeatureDetail
AdminFeatureDetail.description: Summary + recent_votes for a single feature_key (paginated).

AdminVoteRow.title: AdminVoteRow
AdminVoteRow.description:
Single vote with masked email + project name for owner table view.

`user_email_masked` is the masked form (e.g. 'd***@acme.com').
The full email is intentionally NOT included to reduce screenshot
leak risk — the founder console can wire a separate "Reveal"
endpoint later if needed.

AdminVoteSummary.title: AdminVoteSummary
AdminVoteSummary.description:
Aggregate counts + share for one feature_key.

`status` is one of:
  • 'below_threshold'  — interested_pct < ships_after_threshold
  • 'above_threshold'  — interested_pct ≥ ships_after_threshold
  • 'no_votes'         — total == 0

sla_tier.title: Sla Tier
sla_tier.description: Reliability SLA tier: 'none' | 'team' | 'enterprise'. Mutated only by the Founder Console — the lifecycle sweep does NOT reset this on auto-downgrade so refund eligibility audits remain reconstructable.

plan_template.title: Plan Template
plan_template.description: Canonical entitlement template for the current plan_code. Module 6's resolver may override individual values; this is the baseline.

ChangedFilePayload.title: ChangedFilePayload
ChangedFilePayload.description:
Schema-less in transit; the orchestrator's auto-detector tolerates
missing hunks by skipping hunk-rule matches and relying on path rules.

plan_code.title: Plan Code
plan_code.description: Self-serve plan code: 'starter' | 'pro' | 'team'. 'enterprise' is sales-led; 'free' has no checkout.

customer_email.title: Customer Email
customer_email.description: Optional email for Stripe Customer creation when the org has no existing customer_id. Ignored if a customer already exists.

drift.title: Drift
drift.description: older_mean - recent_mean. Positive => the recent half's mean is lower (quality degradation). Negative => improvement.

threshold.title: Threshold
threshold.description: Drift threshold above which we consider degradation material.

breached.title: Breached
breached.description: True if drift > threshold AND both halves have at least the minimum sample count.

DimensionDriftView.title: DimensionDriftView
DimensionDriftView.description: Per-dimension continuous-score drift for one (judge_model, dimension).

FeatureVoteRequest.title: FeatureVoteRequest
FeatureVoteRequest.description:
Body for POST /v1/feature-interest.

`use_case` is the free-text follow-up. Recommended (but not
required) when vote == 'interested' so we learn what fix
customers actually want auto-applied.

FeatureVoteResponse.title: FeatureVoteResponse
FeatureVoteResponse.description:
Returned by POST and GET /v1/feature-interest/me.

No vote counts are exposed — by design (decision: don't bias
customer votes via herd / social proof signal).

median_time_to_resolution_hours_delta.title: Median Time To Resolution Hours Delta

model_context_limit_catalog_updated_at.title: Model Context Limit Catalog Updated At

model_context_limit_catalog_stale_after_.title: Model Context Limit Catalog Stale After Days

IngestEventV2.title: IngestEventV2
IngestEventV2.description: Canonical schema for a single AI call event ingested by Zroky.

payload.title: Payload
payload.description: Parsed payload_json; {} when missing or corrupt

window_hours.title: Window Hours
window_hours.description: Rolling window size (hours) governing every snapshot here.

enabled.title: Enabled
enabled.description: JUDGE_ENABLED setting — when False the engine is dormant.

primary_model.title: Primary Model
primary_model.description: The plan-resolved primary judge for this tenant (single or ensemble median).

ensemble_models.title: Ensemble Models
ensemble_models.description: Ensemble member models when the tenant's plan uses median voting.

verdict_drift.title: Verdict Drift
verdict_drift.description: Per-judge verdict-level drift; empty when no calibration anchors recorded yet.

any_breached.title: Any Breached
any_breached.description: True when at least one verdict or dimension is in breach.

JudgeHealthResponse.title: JudgeHealthResponse
JudgeHealthResponse.description: Aggregate judge-engine health for the calling tenant.

OperatorOverridePayload.title: OperatorOverridePayload
OperatorOverridePayload.description:
Manual blast-radius override from the dashboard.

Validated against `VALID_CATEGORIES`; `target` is free-form (e.g. tool
name). When supplied, this beats every other source — by design, an
operator's deliberate decision overrides both auto-detection and any
declaration in the PR body / .zroky.yml.

call_id.title: Call Id
call_id.description: Zroky call_id to attribute this outcome to.

outcome_type.title: Outcome Type
outcome_type.description: Business event type: refund_issued | ticket_escalated | human_handoff | churn | compliance_fine | retry_cost | custom

amount_usd.title: Amount Usd
amount_usd.description: Monetary cost of this outcome in USD.

occurred_at.title: Occurred At
occurred_at.description: When the event happened (defaults to now).

idempotency_key.title: Idempotency Key
idempotency_key.description: Dedup key — same key + project always returns the same row.

PilotActionRetryResponse.title: PilotActionRetryResponse
PilotActionRetryResponse.description:
Augments the action with the dispatcher decision so the
founder console can render "Retry skipped — same gate failed"
without re-fetching the action row.

PilotPolicyPayload.title: PilotPolicyPayload
PilotPolicyPayload.description:
Strongly-typed mirror of the policy_json schema (plan §6.3).

Defaults intentionally mirror `services.pilot.DEFAULT_POLICY` so a
caller can omit any field and have it default to the canonical value.

provider.title: Provider
provider.description: Provider identifier; must match the migration 0058 CHECK vocab

plaintext_key.title: Plaintext Key
plaintext_key.description: The actual provider API key (never echoed back)

ProviderKeyResponse.title: ProviderKeyResponse
ProviderKeyResponse.description:
Metadata-only wire shape. The dashboard renders identity using
`key_fingerprint` (first 8 chars) + `key_last4`.

RegressionCIRunDetailResponse.title: RegressionCIRunDetailResponse
RegressionCIRunDetailResponse.description:
GET response. `report` and `pr_comment_markdown` are populated
only after the run reaches a terminal state (`pass`/`fail`/`error`).

RegressionCIRunRequest.title: RegressionCIRunRequest
RegressionCIRunRequest.description:
POST body. Every field is optional except `git_sha` (which we
require for audit / dashboard linking — runs without a SHA can't be
tied back to a commit so we reject them at the schema layer).

ReplayDispatchRequest.title: ReplayDispatchRequest
ReplayDispatchRequest.description:
Inbound payload for `POST /v1/replay/dispatch`.

All fields are optional because the GitHub Action's most common
invocation looks like::

    POST /v1/replay/dispatch
    Authorization: Bearer <project-api-key>
    { "git_sha": "abc123", "branch_name": "feature/foo", "pr_number": 42 }

The project's default golden set is resolved server-side when
`golden_set_id` is omitted.

SavingsSummaryResponse.title: SavingsSummaryResponse
SavingsSummaryResponse.description:
Aggregate ROI surface — "what Zroky saved you" across a time window.

Sourced from the legacy `issues` table for the present surface; the
aggregation logic intentionally lives at the route layer so it can be
repointed to `anomalies` once the Phase B migration finishes.

threshold.title: Threshold
threshold.description: Disagreement-rate threshold above which we consider drift breached.

breached.title: Breached
breached.description: True if disagreement_rate > threshold AND min-samples met.

VerdictDriftView.title: VerdictDriftView
VerdictDriftView.description: Verdict-level (pass/fail/inconclusive) calibration drift for one judge.
